days.title: Arrival in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Exploring the vibrant streets

timeline.title: Arrive at Narita International Airport
timeline.detail: Pass through immigration, collect your luggage, and head to the Keisei Skyliner for a convenient ride to the city center.

timeline.title: Check in to Shinjuku Granbell Hotel
timeline.detail: Settle into this modern hotel located in Shinjuku, just a short walk from the nearest subway station and the vibrant nightlife.

timeline.title: Dinner at Ichiran Ramen
timeline.detail: Savor a delicious bowl of tonkotsu ramen at this famous chain. Enjoy the unique dining experience where you can customize your order.

timeline.title: Shibuya Crossing Experience
timeline.detail: Witness the world's busiest pedestrian crossing at Shibuya. It's a vibrant spot filled with energy and a must-see when in Tokyo.

timeline.title: Explore Akihabara
timeline.detail: Stroll through the electric town of Akihabara, famous for its anime and gaming culture. Check out the shops and arcades in the area.

timeline.title: Relax at the Hotel
timeline.detail: After a long day of travel and exploring, unwind in your hotel room and prepare for the upcoming adventures in Tokyo.

localTips: Get a Suica card for convenient subway travel throughout Tokyo.
Try to visit Ichiran during off-peak hours to avoid long queues.
Download a translation app to help with menu reading in restaurants.

days.title: Culinary Delights in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Tasting the best local foods

timeline.title: Breakfast at Tsukiji Outer Market
timeline.detail: Start your day at the bustling Tsukiji Outer Market, sampling fresh sushi and seafood delicacies from various vendors.

timeline.title: Visit the Imperial Palace
timeline.detail: Take a leisurely walk around the beautiful East Gardens of the Imperial Palace, where you can learn about Japan's imperial history.
timeline.mapQuery: Imperial Palace East Gardens, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Lunch at Nakamise Shopping Street
timeline.detail: Indulge in local snacks and street food while wandering through this historic shopping street leading to Senso-ji Temple.
timeline.mapQuery: Nakamise Shopping Street, Asakusa, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Explore Senso-ji Temple
timeline.detail: Visit Tokyo’s oldest temple, Senso-ji, and take in the rich history and culture of this iconic landmark.

timeline.title: Dinner at Katsu Curry Restaurant
timeline.detail: Enjoy a hearty katsu curry at a local restaurant known for its crispy pork cutlet served with flavorful curry sauce.

timeline.title: Shinjuku Nightlife
timeline.detail: Experience the vibrant nightlife of Shinjuku, visiting izakayas and bars in the area for late-night snacks and drinks.

localTips: Visit the Imperial Palace early in the morning to avoid crowds.
Try the melon bread at Nakamise; it’s a local favorite.
Stay hydrated and take breaks; it’s easy to get tired walking around.

days.title: Final Day in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Wrapping up with a culinary adventure

timeline.title: Brunch at Omotesando
timeline.detail: Enjoy a delightful brunch at one of Omotesando’s trendy cafes, known for its unique dishes and beautiful ambiance.

timeline.title: Shopping in Harajuku
timeline.detail: Stroll through the colorful streets of Harajuku, browsing unique shops and enjoying the creative street fashion.

timeline.title: Lunch at a Conveyor Belt Sushi
timeline.detail: Experience the fun of conveyor belt sushi where you can pick and choose dishes as they pass by — a must-try in Tokyo!

timeline.title: Visit Meiji Shrine
timeline.detail: Explore the serene gardens leading to the Meiji Shrine, a peaceful retreat from the bustling city life.

timeline.title: Final Dinner at a Depachika
timeline.detail: Conclude your trip with a dinner at a depachika (department store basement), sampling various high-quality prepared foods on offer.

timeline.title: Return to the Hotel and Rest
timeline.detail: Return to your hotel, pack your belongings, and prepare for your departure the next day.

localTips: Try to shop early in Harajuku to avoid crowds.
Take your time at the depachika to sample different foods.
Make sure to take lots of photos! Tokyo is full of beautiful sights.

hotels.name: Shinjuku Granbell Hotel
hotels.note: Located in the heart of Shinjuku, close to nightlife and dining.
hotels.address: 2 Chome-14-5 Kabukicho, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0021, Japan
